name: phyjax2d

metadata.name: phyjax2d
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# phyjax2d

A jax-based 2d physics library, mainly intended to use for reinforcement learning research.

# License
[Apache LICENSE 2.0](./LICENSE) holds unless otherwise noted.

`vec2d.py` is copied from [PyMunk](pymunk.org) with the license-header as-is.

A convenient builder for creating a space.
Not expected to used with `jax.jit`.

modules.name: phyjax2d.moderngl_vis
documentation:
A simple,  fast visualizer based on moderngl.
Currently, only supports circles and lines.

classes.name: phyjax2d.moderngl_vis.MglVisualizer

Visualizer class that follows the `emevo.Visualizer` protocol.
Considered as a main interface to use this visualizer.

modules.name: phyjax2d.vec2d
documentation:
This module contain the Vec2d class that is used in all of pymunk when a
vector is needed.

The Vec2d class is used almost everywhere in pymunk for 2d coordinates and
vectors, for example to define gravity vector in a space. However, pymunk is
smart enough to convert tuples or tuple like objects to Vec2ds so you usually
do not need to explicitly do conversions if you happen to have a tuple:

```
>>> import pymunk
>>> space = pymunk.Space()
>>> space.gravity
Vec2d(0.0, 0.0)
>>> space.gravity = 3,5
>>> space.gravity
Vec2d(3.0, 5.0)
>>> space.gravity += 2,6
>>> space.gravity
Vec2d(5.0, 11.0)
```

More examples:

```
>>> from pymunk.vec2d import Vec2d
>>> Vec2d(7.3, 4.2)
Vec2d(7.3, 4.2)
>>> Vec2d(7.3, 4.2) + Vec2d(1, 2)
Vec2d(8.3, 6.2)
```

classes.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.__repr__
documentation: String representaion (for debugging)

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.__add__
documentation:
Add a Vec2d with another Vec2d or tuple of size 2

```pycon
>>> Vec2d(3,4) + Vec2d(1,2)
Vec2d(4, 6)
>>> Vec2d(3,4) + (1,2)
Vec2d(4, 6)
```
Addition

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.__radd__
documentation:
Add a tuple of size 2 with a Vec2d

```pycon
>>> (1,2) + Vec2d(3,4)
Vec2d(4, 6)
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.__sub__
documentation:
Subtract a Vec2d with another Vec2d or tuple of size 2

```pycon
>>> Vec2d(3,4) - Vec2d(1,2)
Vec2d(2, 2)
>>> Vec2d(3,4) - (1,2)
Vec2d(2, 2)
```
Subtraction

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.__rsub__
documentation:
Subtract a tuple of size 2 with a Vec2d

```pycon
>>> (1,2) - Vec2d(3,4)
Vec2d(-2, -2)
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.__mul__
documentation:
Multiply with a float

```pycon
>>> Vec2d(3,6) * 2.5
Vec2d(7.5, 15.0)
```
Multiplication

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.__rmul__
documentation:
Multiply a float with a Vec2d

```pycon
>>> 2.5 * Vec2d(3,6)
Vec2d(7.5, 15.0)
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.__floordiv__
documentation:
Floor division by a float (also known as integer division)

```pycon
>>> Vec2d(3,6) // 2.0
Vec2d(1.0, 3.0)
```
Division

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.__truediv__
documentation:
Division by a float

```pycon
>>> Vec2d(3,6) / 2.0
Vec2d(1.5, 3.0)
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.__neg__
documentation:
Return the negated version of the Vec2d

```pycon
>>> -Vec2d(1,-2)
Vec2d(-1, 2)
```
Unary operations

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.__pos__
documentation:
Return the unary pos of the Vec2d.

```pycon
>>> +Vec2d(1,-2)
Vec2d(1, -2)
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.__abs__
documentation:
Return the length of the Vec2d

```pycon
>>> abs(Vec2d(3,4))
5.0
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.get_length_sqrd
documentation:
Get the squared length of the vector.
If the squared length is enough it is more efficient to use this method
instead of first calling get_length() or access .length and then do a
x\*\*2.

```pycon
>>> v = Vec2d(3,4)
>>> v.get_length_sqrd() == v.length**2
True
```

```
return

The squared length
```
vectory functions

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.length
documentation:
Get the length of the vector.

```pycon
>>> Vec2d(10, 0).length
10.0
>>> '%.2f' % Vec2d(10, 20).length
'22.36'
```

```
return

The length
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.scale_to_length
documentation:
Return a copy of this vector scaled to the given length.

```pycon
>>> '%.2f, %.2f' % Vec2d(10, 20).scale_to_length(20)
'8.94, 17.89'
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.rotated
documentation:
Create and return a new vector by rotating this vector by
angle_radians radians.

```
return

Rotated vector
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.rotated_degrees
documentation:
Create and return a new vector by rotating this vector by
angle_degrees degrees.

```
return

Rotade vector
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.angle
documentation: The angle (in radians) of the vector

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.angle_degrees
documentation: Gets the angle (in degrees) of a vector

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.get_angle_between
documentation:
Get the angle between the vector and the other in radians

```
return

The angle
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.get_angle_degrees_between
documentation:
Get the angle between the vector and the other in degrees

```
return

The angle (in degrees)
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.normalized
documentation:
Get a normalized copy of the vector
Note: This function will return 0 if the length of the vector is 0.

```
return

A normalized vector
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.normalized_and_length
documentation:
Normalize the vector and return its length before the normalization

```
return

The length before the normalization
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.dot
documentation:
The dot product between the vector and other vector

: v1.dot(v2) -> v1.x\*v2.x + v1.y\*v2.y

```
return

The dot product
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.get_distance
documentation:
The distance between the vector and other vector

```
return

The distance
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.get_dist_sqrd
documentation:
The squared distance between the vector and other vector
It is more efficent to use this method than to call get_distance()
first and then do a sqrt() on the result.

```
return

The squared distance
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.projection
documentation: Project this vector on top of other vector

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.cross
documentation:
The cross product between the vector and other vector

: v1.cross(v2) -> v1.x\*v2.y - v1.y\*v2.x

```
return

The cross product
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.int_tuple
documentation:
The x and y values of this vector as a tuple of ints.
Uses round() to round to closest int.

```pycon
>>> Vec2d(0.9, 2.4).int_tuple
(1, 2)
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.zero
documentation:
A vector of zero length.

```pycon
>>> Vec2d.zero()
Vec2d(0, 0)
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.unit
documentation:
A unit vector pointing up

```pycon
>>> Vec2d.unit()
Vec2d(0, 1)
```

methods.name: phyjax2d.vec2d.Vec2d.ones
documentation:
A vector where both x and y is 1

```pycon
>>> Vec2d.ones()
Vec2d(1, 1)
```

2d vector class, supports vector and scalar operators, and also
provides some high level functions.

functions.name: phyjax2d.impl.apply_velocity_normal
documentation:
Apply velocity constraints to the solver.
Suppose that each shape has (N_contact, 1) or (N_contact, 2).

functions.name: phyjax2d.impl.apply_bounce
documentation:
Apply bounce (resititution).
Suppose that each shape has (N_contact, 1) or (N_contact, 2).

functions.name: phyjax2d.impl.correct_position
documentation:
Correct positions to remove penetration.
Suppose that each shape in contact and helper has (N_contact, 1) or (N_contact, 2).
p1 and p2 should have xy: (1, 2) angle (1, 1) shape

functions.name: phyjax2d.impl.solve_constraints
documentation: Resolve collisions by Sequential Impulse method
